source_jobs.title: Design Engineer (UI/UX)
source_jobs.description:
TAKE COMPLEX, DATA-DENSE PROFESSIONAL SOFTWARE AND MAKE IT FEEL EFFORTLESS — AND TREAT DOCUMENTING IT IN FIGMA AS PART OF THE CRAFT, NOT AN AFTERTHOUGHT.




HEY THERE — WE’RE KINSHIP 👋

We make software for architects and engineering teams. The work they do is genuinely complex — huge libraries of project content, mountains of data, fiddly technical detail — and most of the tools in their world are clunky and joyless. Ours aren't: our whole reason for existing is to take that complexity and make it feel clean, fast, and a pleasure to use.

That's the design challenge, and it's a great one. We're looking for a product designer who lives and breathes UI — someone who turns dense, complicated workflows into interfaces that feel clean, obvious, and quietly delightful. This isn't about making pretty pictures; it's about understanding how people actually work, shaping the right solution, and documenting it with real care. You don't need to know a thing about architecture or engineering to start — we'll bring you up to speed on what matters; what counts is that you're brilliant at making complex software feel simple.

And here's the best part: we already have a great designer (no joke). So you won't be a lone designer holding everything together — you'd be joining to help us grow the design team, raise the bar together, and shape how design works at Kinship as we scale. If you're properly fluent in Figma — the kind of person who treats a well-built component library and a clean handoff as a craft in itself — you'll feel right at home.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Own the design of features end to end — from the problem and the flows through to pixel-perfect, developer-ready UI.

 - Turn complex, data-heavy workflows into interfaces that feel simple, fast, and genuinely helpful.

 - Build and evolve our design system in Figma — components, variants, tokens, and patterns that keep the product consistent as it grows.

 - Document your work properly — states, edge cases, and interactions all spelled out — so building it is the easy part.

 - Prototype interactions to pressure-test motion and behaviour before anyone writes code.

 - Work hand in hand with our engineers, and help grow and shape the design team as we scale.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You don't need to tick every box, but here's what would help you succeed.


MUST-HAVES

 - 5+ years designing software products — real applications with structured data and rich interactions — not just websites or marketing pages — with a portfolio that shows shipped work, systems thinking, and obsessive attention to detail.

 - Mastery of Figma as both a design and a documentation tool. Components, variants, auto-layout, variables/tokens, named states — and handoff that's clean, complete, and easy to build from. (If your home is Sketch instead, that's welcome too — it's the next best thing.)

 - Figma files you can walk us through. We care as much about how you document and hand off as we do about the final screens — so expect to show us the inside of a file, not just the pretty frames.

 - A deep eye for the details that make an interface just work: typography, spacing, hierarchy, and the unglamorous states — empty, loading, error, edge cases.

 - Experience collaborating closely with engineers in a product team — clear communication, thoughtful handoff, and the patience to iterate until it's right.


NICE-TO-HAVES (THESE WON'T GATE YOU)

 - Comfort with Framer (or similar) for higher-fidelity interaction prototypes — to show how something should move, not to hand off.

 - Comfort reading and tweaking HTML & CSS — enough to understand how your designs become real interfaces, and to nudge things in the browser when it helps. You absolutely don't need to be a coder, and we won't screen anyone out for not being one. But if you're a designer who genuinely loves dipping into code, you'll have a brilliant time here.

 - Experience building or owning a design system.




HOW WE THINK ABOUT DESI

source_jobs.title: Product Specialist, Revit
source_jobs.description:
HELP US TURN ROUGH IDEAS INTO BRILLIANT, ROCK SOLID FEATURES.




HEY THERE — WE’RE KINSHIP 👋

We’re building the content management and project tracking platform for Revit that AEC firms actually want to use — because we’ve lived the pain ourselves. Our tools help architects, engineers, and BIM managers standardize libraries, track usage, and eliminate chaos.

We’re looking for a Product Specialist to join our team in London. You’ll be the person who makes sure our tools don’t just look good, but actually work the way they should. From testing new features to uncovering hidden bugs, you’ll work with our engineers to help ensure every release is polished, dependable, and ready for real-world use.

You’ll also work closely with our customers to provide technical support and help them understand what Kinship can do and how to get the most out of it.

This is a hands-on role for someone who lives and breathes Revit, enjoys digging into technical details, and wants to be involved in both product and people.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Help test new product features — from early concept to final QA

 - Manage customer help tickets, ensuring issues get resolved quickly and customers feel supported along the way

 - Deliver onboarding, demos, and technical walkthroughs to new and existing users

 - Write support content, workflows, and best practices to help people succeed with Kinship




WE THINK YOUR BACKGROUND PROBABLY INCLUDES:

 - 3+ years of professional experience with Revit — ideally in architecture, engineering, or BIM coordination roles

 - A solid understanding of Revit family and project content, workflows, and common challenges

 - Experience creating documentation, tutorials, or training materials for Revit users

 - Familiarity with QA workflows — even better if you’ve done structured testing or used tools like GitHub Issues, TestRail, Qase, or Testmo




YOU’RE PROBABLY SOMEONE WHO:

 - Is great at spotting the little things and loves making workflows smoother

 - Can explain technical ideas clearly — whether you’re talking to a developer or a project lead

 - Enjoys solving problems for users and figuring out what really works (not just what sounds good)

 - Likes working across functions — from product and design to engineering and support

 - Takes pride in making things clearer, faster, and better for others




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary

 - A beautiful, plant-filled office in central London (by Southwark tube station) and good coffee (obviously)

 - A no-BS team of people who love building great software together

 - A company that believes in work-life balance

source_jobs.title: Revit Content Specialist
source_jobs.description:
BUILD THE FAMILIES THAT REVIT TEAMS LOVE TO USE




HEY THERE — WE’RE KINSHIP 👋

Our platform helps architects and engineers manage Revit content, improve workflows, and bring more clarity to complex projects. We also create high-quality Revit families — some shared freely with our customers and the wider industry, others developed specifically for manufacturers.

We’re hiring a Revit Content Specialist to help us design and build that content — families that are clean, beautiful, and genuinely usable. This isn’t about cranking out geometry — it’s about crafting content that works the way real users need it to, with attention to structure, performance, and clarity.

If you’ve built families that people love (and don’t swear at), understand lookup tables, formula-driven parameters, family templates inside and out, and know your way around nested families, this might be your dream job.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Create high-quality Revit families for real-world use across a variety of disciplines.

 - Ensure content is clean, consistent, well-structured, and easy to use.

 - Work with our content team to define and maintain standards for naming, parameters, nesting, visibility, and performance.

 - Collaborate with customers and internal teams to understand project needs and deliver content that fits seamlessly.

 - Maintain and update existing content based on feedback, new requirements, or Revit versioning.

 - Contribute to internal tools and processes for content QA, automation, and library management.




WE THINK YOUR BACKGROUND PROBABLY INCLUDES:

 - 3+ years of hands-on Revit experience, with a focus on family creation

 - A deep understanding of how families are used in real-world project workflows

 - Strong working knowledge of shared parameters, types, visibility settings, and nesting

 - Experience with both hosted and non-hosted families, face-based, line-based, etc

 - Familiarity with Revit content standards — any or multiple — is a plus

 - Comfort reviewing and working with customer-specific content libraries and standards




YOU’RE PROBABLY SOMEONE WHO:

 - Can spot a bad parameter name a mile off

 - Has a builder’s mindset — you care about function, clarity, and performance

 - Likes making complex things simple (especially for the person using your content)

 - Enjoys refining and improving how things are built — not just what they do

 - Appreciates precision and detail, but knows when good enough is good enough

 - Wants to work in-person with a tight, focused team of Revit nerds, engineers, and product people




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary

 - A beautiful, plant-filled office in central London (by Southwark tube station) and good coffee (obviously)

 - A no-BS team of people who love building great software, and incredible, beautiful Revit families, together

 - A company that believes in work-life balance and actually lives it

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer, C#/.NET
source_jobs.description:
POWERING BETTER REVIT WORKFLOWS FROM THE INSIDE OUT




HEY THERE — WE’RE KINSHIP 👋

We’re building tools that make working with Revit simpler, faster, and a whole lot less painful. Our platform helps architects and engineers manage their content libraries, track how models evolve, and enforce better standards without the usual chaos.

We’re hiring a C# developer to help us go deeper into the world of Revit — improving our add-in, scaling sync capabilities, and crafting the behind-the-scenes magic that makes Kinship feel seamless to our users. If you know your way around Revit’s quirks and love writing clean, solid code that solves real-world problems, this is for you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Develop and maintain our Revit add-in using C#

 - Implement new features and improvements within the Revit environment

 - Integrate the add-in with backend services via REST APIs

 - Handle data synchronization between Revit and our cloud platform

 - Contribute to architectural decisions and collaborate with other engineers on design and implementation




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

You don’t need to tick every box, but here’s what would help you succeed:


MUST-HAVES

 - 3–6+ years of experience writing C#

 - Strong hands-on experience with developing desktop application

 - Solid MVVM knowledge (using CommunityToolkit.Mvvm)

 - Experience with WPF or WinUI 3 (including styles, templates, and resource dictionaries)

 - Confident with async programming and writing responsive, stable desktop apps


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Experience with the Revit API, or similar

 - Experience with dependency injection

 - Familiarity with build tools like Nuke and CI/CD pipelines

 - Understanding of mapping tools (e.g., Mapperly, AutoMapper)

 - You write unit tests and know when they matter

 - Experience with structured logging (Serilog preferred)

 - Familiarity with observability tools like OpenTelemetry

 - Some experience with ASP.NET http://ASP.NET and how web APIs are built




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary

 - A beautiful, plant-filled office in central London (by Southwark tube station) and good coffee (obviously)

 - A no-BS team of people who love building great software together

 - Real autonomy and influence over product and tech decisions

 - A company that believes in work-life balance

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer, Frontend
source_jobs.description:
BUILD FAST, ACCESSIBLE, RESILIENT INTERFACES THAT MAKE WORKING IN REVIT A PLEASURE. FOR ENGINEERS WHO CARE ABOUT THE WEB ITSELF, NOT JUST THE FRAMEWORK OF THE MONTH.




HEY THERE — WE’RE KINSHIP 👋

We're building the Revit tools you wish existed. Our platform helps architects and engineers manage content, track usage, and make smarter decisions with less hassle.

We're looking for a frontend engineer who cares about the web itself. Someone who reaches for HTML and CSS first, treats JavaScript as something you add on purpose rather than by default, and judges their work by what users get: interfaces that are fast, accessible, resilient, and a pleasure to use.

We build with React today, and you'll work in it daily. We're not asking anyone to come in and rewrite everything from scratch. But we care far more about how well you understand the platform underneath than about which tools you're fluent in. Frameworks come and go; the web is versionless, and a real command of it compounds over an entire career.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Own user-facing features (advanced search, model usage dashboards, sync-rule management, interactive content previews) and sweat the details that make them feel effortless

 - Reach for the right tool for each problem: native HTML elements and modern CSS where they do the job well, React where it earns its place

 - Treat performance and accessibility as first-class outcomes, not afterthoughts: quick on a mid-range device and a flaky connection, usable with a keyboard and a screen reader

 - Build and maintain shared component libraries and a frontend architecture

 - Tackle real frontend challenges: complex state, large datasets, keeping the UI fast and responsive across the app

 - Work closely with designers, engineers and Revit experts




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You don’t need to tick every box, but here’s what would help you succeed:


MUST-HAVES

 - A deep, hands-on command of HTML and CSS: semantics, layout, and modern CSS (cascade layers, container queries, :has(), logical properties, oklch(), whatever the job needs), plus an eye for spacing, states, and the details that make an interface just work

 - Fluency in JavaScript/TypeScript and React, comfortable in a modern component-based codebase and able to tell when a problem needs JavaScript at all

 - A habit of progressive enhancement: build something that works, then layer on the nice-to-haves, so a slow network or an old device degrades gracefully instead of breaking

 - Hands-on in the browser's DevTools as a real debugging instrument, not just a place to dump console.logs. You can use the Performance panel to find what's blocking the main thread, read a network waterfall, throttle CPU and network to reproduce a user's bad day, and inspect the DOM and computed styles to track down a layout bug

 - Several years (think 5+) building real things for the web, and experience reasoning about complex UI systems and state across a real app

 - A strong eye for design and interaction, and a real appreciation for good UX

 - You question your defaults and don't pull in a library or reach for a framework feature just because it's there, and you can explain the calls you made


NICE-TO-HAVES

 - Web-performance chops (Core Web Vitals, real-user vs. lab data, the cost of JavaScript on low-end devices)

 - Accessibility experience (WCAG, testing with a screen reader)

 - Familiarity with the modern frontend ecosystem (Tailwind, Zustand, Radix, Framer Motion); useful, never the point

 - Experience with Revit, 3D, or AEC tools




HOW WE THINK ABOUT THE WEB

We get excited about what the browser can already do. We'd rather use a view transition in a few lines of CSS than ship a single-page app to fake one. We think doing less work is usually faster than doing more, that every layer of abstraction is something you then have to maintain, and that the platform (versionless, resilient, quietly getting better while you sleep) is the safest thing to bui

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer, Backend
source_jobs.description:
CRAFTING THE KIND OF BACKEND THAT MAKES FRONTEND WORK FEEL LIKE CHEATING




HEY THERE — WE’RE KINSHIP 👋

We’re building the Revit tools you wish existed. Our platform helps architects and engineers manage content, track usage, and make smarter decisions with less hassle.

We’re looking for a backend-focused engineer who enjoys building clean APIs, designing resilient systems, and keeping things fast, simple, and secure. Someone who’s comfortable working in PHP, knows their way around Docker, and can help us make the most of AWS to ship reliable software at speed.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build and maintain backend services in modern PHP

 - Design and implement APIs consumed by our frontend and desktop apps

 - Use Docker to standardize development and streamline deployments

 - Work with AWS services (e.g. ECS, S3, RDS, CloudWatch) to keep our systems fast and reliable

 - Collaborate with product, design, and other engineers to ship features

 - Write tests and monitor performance so we can trust our stack at scale




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

You don’t need to tick every box, but here’s what would help you succeed:

 - Strong experience with PHP (we use PHP 8+)

 - Familiarity with containerized environments using Docker

 - Product mindset — you think in terms of user value, not just technical correctness

 - Clear communicator and thoughtful collaborator

 - Someone who’s excited to work in a small, fast-moving team

 - Knowledge of Laravel or Symfony

 - Comfortable with AWS basics — think deploying apps, storing files, managing databases

 - Experience designing or evolving APIs in production

 - Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines

 - Interest in DevOps, observability, or developer tooling




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary

 - A beautiful, plant-filled office in central London (by Southwark tube station) and good coffee (obviously)

 - A no-BS team of people who love building great software together

 - Real autonomy and influence over product and tech decisions

 - A company that believes in work-life balance

source_jobs.title: Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
SHAPE HOW AN ENTIRE INDUSTRY UNDERSTANDS KINSHIP




HEY THERE – WE'RE KINSHIP 👋

We’re building the Revit tools that architects and engineers actually want to use. Our platform helps teams manage their content, track project data, and make smarter decisions without the usual chaos.

People across the industry know the Kinship name. What they don’t always know is what Kinship actually is. That’s where you come in.

We’re looking for a Marketing Lead to own how Kinship shows up in the world—our messaging, content, and brand.

This isn’t a role where you’re handed a playbook. It’s a chance to define one, turn a clear strategic vision into consistent execution, and build a brand that feels confident, credible, and quietly different from everything else in the AEC space.

If you care deeply about clarity, taste, and craft—and you enjoy owning problems end to end—you’ll feel at home here.



IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Own Kinship’s marketing execution end to end, across our website, blog, LinkedIn, YouTube, and email

 - Turn product features and releases into clear, compelling narratives that people actually understand

 - Drive inbound lead generation through consistent, high-quality content (no outbound or cold calling)

 - Shape and maintain Kinship’s brand voice: confident, concise, and refreshingly un-corporate

 - Decide what gets published, when, and why—balancing long-term brand building with short-term impact


YOU’LL SPEND YOUR TIME ON THINGS LIKE:

 - Keeping the website up to date as a living product asset—not a static brochure

 - Running a steady content cadence (weekly blog posts, daily(ish) social posts, monthly newsletters)

 - Leading pain-point-focused content campaigns based on real Revit and workflow frustrations

 - Working closely with the team to turn deep technical knowledge into strong, readable content

 - Supporting high-quality video content that shows Kinship in action

 - Identifying and pursuing smart opportunities for PR, podcasts, events, and industry visibility


WE THINK YOUR BACKGROUND PROBABLY INCLUDES:

 - Several years (ideally 5+) working in B2B, SaaS, or technical product marketing

 - Experience being the most senior marketer in a small or growing team

 - Strong writing and editing instincts—you know when something is clear and compelling

 - Comfort owning strategy and execution, without layers of process

 - Experience working closely with product and sales/CS teams


YOU’RE PROBABLY SOMEONE WHO:

 - Enjoys figuring things out when there isn’t a clear answer yet

 - Cares about tone, structure, and clarity—not just volume or reach

 - Is allergic to generic LinkedIn marketing and buzzwords

 - Likes building things properly, even if it takes a little longer

 - Wants real ownership and influence, not just a title


WHAT WE OFFER:

 - Competitive salary (based on experience and location)

 - A beautiful, plant-filled office in central London (by Southwark tube station) and good coffee (obviously)

 - A no-BS team of people who love building great software together

 - The chance to shape a brand—and a category—while it’s still being defined

 - A company that insists on work-life balance

source_jobs.title: Junior Product Specialist, Revit
source_jobs.description:
HELP US TURN ROUGH IDEAS INTO BRILLIANT, ROCK SOLID FEATURES.




HEY THERE — WE’RE KINSHIP 👋

We’re building the content management and project tracking platform for Revit that AEC firms actually want to use — because we’ve lived the pain ourselves. Our tools help architects, engineers, and BIM managers standardize libraries, track usage, and eliminate chaos.

We’re looking for a Junior Product Specialist to join our team in London. You’ll be the person who makes sure our tools don’t just look good, but actually work the way they should. From testing new features to uncovering hidden bugs, you’ll work with our engineers to help ensure every release is polished, dependable, and ready for real-world use.

You’ll also work closely with our customers to provide technical support and help them understand what Kinship can do and how to get the most out of it.

This is a hands-on role for someone who loves software, enjoys digging into technical details, and wants to be involved in both product and people.




IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Help test new product features — from early concept to final QA

 - Answer customer help tickets, ensuring issues get resolved quickly and customers feel supported along the way

 - Write support content and documentation to help people succeed with Kinship

 - Learn all about Revit and the AEC industry




WE THINK YOUR BACKGROUND PROBABLY INCLUDES:

 - Some experience providing technical support, whether professionally or simply as the most techie person people know

 - Experience communicating technical concepts in plain English

 - A demonstrated ability to quickly learn and become proficient in new technologies

 - Bonus points if you have some previous exposure to the architecture, engineering or construction fields




YOU’RE PROBABLY SOMEONE WHO:

 - Is great at spotting the little things and loves making workflows smoother

 - Can explain technical ideas clearly in writing

 - Enjoys solving problems for users and figuring out what really works

 - Loves learning about new technologies and applications

 - Likes working across functions — from product and design to engineering and support

 - Takes pride in making things clearer, faster, and better for others




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Competitive salary (£24,000-£28,000 per year)

 - A beautiful, plant-filled office in central London (by Southwark tube station) and good coffee (obviously)

 - A no-BS team of people who love building great software together

 - A company that believes in work-life balance

notes: Kinship is a product-heavy company with a strong focus on Revit tools and content management. Most job postings are for product engineers, designers, and specialists.
